title: Neural Scaling & Emergence Shadow Analysis

scaling_law_approach.description: L(N) ~ A·N^{-α} approaching threshold: smooth EML-2 regime
scaling_law_approach.why: Power law L(N) = exp(-α log N): EML-2; smooth until threshold

emergence_threshold.shadow_analysis: The emergence event itself is EML-∞ (discontinuous phase transition). But the shadow — the best constructive approximation — is EML-2: the scaling law L(N) that extrapolates toward the threshold. The shadow does NOT predict when emergence occurs, but it is EML-2.

loss_curve.description: L_train ~ 0, L_test ~ exp(-γt): delayed exponential decay in generalization
loss_curve.why: exp(-γt) = EML-1, but with normalization log Z = EML-2

test_loss_curve.description: L_test(N/P) with peak at N=P: double U-shape
test_loss_curve.why: Power law structure in both regimes: EML-2

effective_df.description: Effective degrees of freedom df(λ) = Tr(X(X^TX+λI)^{-1}X^T)
effective_df.why: Trace of resolvent: spectral measure integral = EML-2

phase_transitions.object: Sharp phase transitions in large language models

in_context_learning.description: ICL emerges discontinuously: attention pattern = exp(q·k^T)/Z
in_context_learning.why: Softmax attention: exp(q·k^T) with normalization Z = log-partition = EML-2. But PHASE TRANSITION in ICL: attention head specializes = complex pattern = EML-3

chain_of_thought.description: CoT emergence: multi-step reasoning appears suddenly
chain_of_thought.why: CoT = sequential composition of EML-2 steps, but the COMPOSITION itself jumps from EML-2 to EML-3 level (oscillatory search in reasoning space). Attention over long contexts: exp(i·position) rotary encoding = EML-3

rotary_position_encoding.description: RoPE: q·k = Re(q·k̄) where q=q_e^{imθ}: complex rotation
rotary_position_encoding.why: exp(imθ): winding number in position space = complex exponential = EML-3

smooth_emergences.description: Capabilities that scale smoothly with N

ring_closure.object: Scaling law ring closure (EML-2 closed subring)
ring_closure.note: Scaling laws themselves are EML-2 (not EML-∞); their ring closure = EML-2
ring_closure.ring_shadow_connection: The EML-2 closure of scaling laws (from S254/S258) means: any CONSTRUCTIVE approach within the scaling ring stays EML-2. Emergence exits the ring and enters EML-∞ — shadow of EML-∞ emergence = EML-2 or EML-3 depending on whether the transition is real-valued (EML-2) or phase-carrying (EML-3).

key_theorem: The Neural Emergence Shadow Rule (S265): Emergence events split by shadow type based on WHAT capability emerges: EML-2 shadow (measurement): smooth capability scaling, grokking, double descent —   all approached by power-law/logistic curves = real exponential = EML-2. EML-3 shadow (oscillation): sharp discontinuous emergences (ICL, CoT) —   the attention mechanism involves complex rotations (RoPE, exp(imθ)),   and the sharp transition involves phase-locking in the attention heads = EML-3. The smooth/sharp distinction IS the EML-2/EML-3 shadow distinction: smooth = real-exponential approach (EML-2); sharp = complex-phase transition (EML-3). PREDICTION: any emergent capability that can be plotted on a smooth curve with model scale has EML-2 shadow; any capability with a discontinuous onset has EML-3 shadow. This is testable: check whether the best approximation to the transition uses real-valued (EML-2) or complex-phase (EML-3) mathematical tools.
rabbit_hole_log: Smooth emergence → EML-2 shadow: scaling law L(N) is real power law
Sharp emergence (ICL, CoT) → EML-3 shadow: attention uses exp(imθ) rotary encoding
RoPE (rotary position encoding): exp(imθ) = complex oscillation; sharp emergence = EML-3
Prediction: smooth capability = EML-2 shadow; discontinuous capability = EML-3 shadow
